annotations.label: Tetrahedron

anchor.snippet: a polyhedron composed of four triangular faces

annotations.note: Tetrahedra are not defined as polyhedra in Mathlib, but the 3-simplex `Affine.Simplex ℝ P 3` captures the affine notion.

annotations.label: 3-simplex

anchor.snippet: the three-dimensional case of the more general concept of a Euclidean simplex

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `Affine.Simplex k P n` for any `n`, with `n = 3` being the 3-simplex.

annotations.label: Triangular pyramid

anchor.snippet: is one kind of pyramid , which is a polyhedron with a flat polygon base and triangular faces connecting the base to a common point

annotations.note: There is no notion of pyramid as a polyhedron in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Two nets

anchor.snippet: a tetrahedron can be folded from a single sheet of paper. It has two such nets

annotations.note: Polyhedral nets are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Existence of circumsphere and insphere

anchor.snippet: there exists a sphere (called the circumsphere ) on which all four vertices lie, and another sphere (the insphere ) tangent to the tetrahedron's faces

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `Affine.Simplex.circumsphere` and `Affine.Simplex.insphere` for any simplex, including the 3-simplex.

annotations.label: Regular tetrahedron

anchor.snippet: A regular tetrahedron is a tetrahedron in which all four faces are equilateral triangles

annotations.note: `Affine.Simplex.Regular` (and `Equilateral`) for `n = 3` give the regular tetrahedron.

annotations.label: Deltahedron

anchor.snippet: the simplest deltahedron , a polyhedron all of whose faces are equilateral triangles

annotations.note: Deltahedra are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Orthocentric tetrahedron

anchor.snippet: If all three pairs of opposite edges of a tetrahedron are perpendicular , then it is called an orthocentric tetrahedron

annotations.note: Orthocentric tetrahedra are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Semi-orthocentric tetrahedron

anchor.snippet: When only one pair of opposite edges are perpendicular, it is called a semi-orthocentric tetrahedron

annotations.note: Semi-orthocentric tetrahedra are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Trirectangular tetrahedron

anchor.snippet: In a trirectangular tetrahedron the three face angles at one vertex are right angles

annotations.note: Trirectangular tetrahedra are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Isodynamic tetrahedron

anchor.snippet: An isodynamic tetrahedron is one in which the cevians that join the vertices to the incenters of the opposite faces are concurrent

annotations.note: Isodynamic tetrahedra are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Isogonic tetrahedron

anchor.snippet: An isogonic tetrahedron has concurrent cevians that join the vertices to the points of contact of the opposite faces with the inscribed sphere

annotations.note: Isogonic tetrahedra are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Disphenoid

anchor.snippet: A disphenoid is a tetrahedron with four congruent triangles as faces

annotations.note: Disphenoids are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: 3-orthoscheme

anchor.snippet: A 3-orthoscheme is a tetrahedron where all four faces are right triangles

annotations.note: Orthoschemes are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Orthoscheme

anchor.snippet: An orthoscheme is an irregular simplex that is the convex hull of a tree in which all edges are mutually perpendicular

annotations.note: General orthoschemes are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Cube dissected into 3-orthoschemes

anchor.snippet: the cube can be subdivided into instances of this orthoscheme

annotations.note: Cube dissection into orthoschemes is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Regular tetrahedron subdivision into characteristic tetrahedra

anchor.snippet: The regular tetrahedron is subdivided into 24 instances of its characteristic tetrahedron by its planes of symmetry

annotations.note: This subdivision result is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Characteristic tetrahedron edge lengths

anchor.snippet: If the regular tetrahedron has edge length

annotations.note: Characteristic tetrahedron edge length formulas are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Space-filling tetrahedron

anchor.snippet: A space-filling tetrahedron packs with directly congruent or enantiomorphous

annotations.note: Space-filling tetrahedra are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Scissors-congruence to a cube

anchor.snippet: All space-filling tetrahedra are scissors-congruent to a cube

annotations.note: Scissors congruence is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Regular tetrahedra do not fill space

anchor.snippet: Regular tetrahedra, however, cannot fill space by themselves

annotations.note: This space-filling impossibility result is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Goursat tetrahedron

anchor.snippet: An irregular tetrahedron which is the fundamental domain

annotations.note: Goursat tetrahedra (in this geometric sense) are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Longest Edge Bisection

anchor.snippet: One of the commonly used subdivision methods is the Longest Edge Bisection

annotations.note: Longest Edge Bisection is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Similarity class

anchor.snippet: A similarity class is the set of tetrahedra with the same geometric shape

annotations.note: Mathlib has a notion of simplex similarity in `Mathlib.Geometry.Euclidean.Similarity`, but does not bundle similarity classes as a set.

annotations.label: Iterative LEB similarity classes

anchor.snippet: The iterative LEB of the regular tetrahedron has been shown to produce only 8 similarity classes

annotations.note: The iterative LEB similarity-class theorem is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Tetrahedral graph is a wheel graph

anchor.snippet: the tetrahedral graph is a wheel graph

annotations.note: Neither the tetrahedral graph nor wheel graphs appear to be defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: No space diagonal

anchor.snippet: The tetrahedron is one of the polyhedra that does not have space diagonal

annotations.note: Space diagonals of polyhedra are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Self-dual

anchor.snippet: It is self-dual , meaning its dual polyhedron is a tetrahedron itself

annotations.note: Polyhedron duality is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Volume of a tetrahedron

anchor.snippet: The volume of a tetrahedron is given by the formula

annotations.note: A general (1/3)·base·height volume formula for tetrahedra is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Volume via determinant

anchor.snippet: In terms of a determinant , the volume of a tetrahedron is

annotations.note: The determinant volume formula for tetrahedra is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Volume via scalar triple product

anchor.snippet: another approach is by the absolute value of the scalar triple product

annotations.note: Although the scalar/triple product exists in `Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.CrossProduct`, the connection to tetrahedron volume is not formalized.

annotations.label: Cayley–Menger determinant volume

anchor.snippet: Given the distances between the vertices of a tetrahedron the volume can be computed using the Cayley–Menger determinant

annotations.note: The Cayley–Menger determinant is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Volume from six edge lengths

anchor.snippet: The above formula uses six lengths of edges, and the following formula uses three lengths of edges and three angles

annotations.note: Tetrahedron-volume edge/angle formulas are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Heron-type volume formula

anchor.snippet: The volume of a tetrahedron can be ascertained by using the Heron formula

annotations.note: A Heron-type volume formula for tetrahedra is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Bimedian bisects volume

anchor.snippet: Any plane containing a bimedian (connector of opposite edges' midpoints) of a tetrahedron bisects the volume of the tetrahedron

annotations.note: Bimedians and volume-bisection theorems are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Murakami–Yano formula

anchor.snippet: the volume is given by the Murakami–Yano formula

annotations.note: The Murakami–Yano formula is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Volume via skew-line distance

anchor.snippet: another formula for the volume of a tetrahedron

annotations.note: Volume via skew-line distances is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Monge point

anchor.snippet: now known as the Monge point : the point where the six midplanes of a tetrahedron intersect

annotations.note: `Affine.Simplex.mongePoint` defines the Monge point for any simplex (including tetrahedra).

annotations.label: Midplane

anchor.snippet: A midplane is defined as a plane that is orthogonal to an edge joining any two vertices

annotations.note: Midplanes are not explicitly defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Median and bimedian

anchor.snippet: A line segment joining a vertex of a tetrahedron with the centroid of the opposite face is called a median

annotations.note: Mathlib has `faceOppositeCentroid` so the median's endpoints are available, but medians and bimedians are not defined as segments.

annotations.label: Concurrency at centroid

anchor.snippet: These seven line segments are all concurrent at a point called the centroid of the tetrahedron

annotations.note: The centroid is defined, but full concurrency of medians/bimedians at the centroid is not packaged as a theorem.

annotations.label: Medians 3:1 ratio

anchor.snippet: the four medians are divided in a 3:1 ratio by the centroid

annotations.note: `dist (s.points i) s.centroid = n * dist s.centroid (s.faceOppositeCentroid i)` specializes to the 3:1 ratio at `n = 3`.

annotations.label: Euler line of tetrahedron

anchor.snippet: These points define the Euler line of the tetrahedron

annotations.note: The Euler line of a tetrahedron/simplex is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Twelve-point sphere

anchor.snippet: It is the twelve-point sphere and besides the centroids of the four faces

annotations.note: Mathlib's `ninePointCircle` is the 3(n+1)-point sphere of a simplex; for `n = 3` it is the twelve-point sphere of a tetrahedron.

annotations.label: Twelve-point sphere radius

anchor.snippet: The radius of the twelve-point sphere is one-third of the circumradius of the reference tetrahedron

annotations.note: `ninePointCircle_radius` gives `circumradius / n`, which is `circumradius / 3` for the tetrahedron.

annotations.label: Relation among face angles

anchor.snippet: There is a relation among the angles made by the faces of a general tetrahedron

annotations.note: Relations among tetrahedral dihedral/face angles are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Isogonic center (Lindelöf)

anchor.snippet: Lorenz Lindelöf found that, corresponding to any given tetrahedron is a point now known as an isogonic center

annotations.note: Lindelöf's isogonic center for tetrahedra is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Law of sines for tetrahedron

anchor.section: Trigonometry and the space of all shapes
anchor.snippet: A corollary of the usual law of sines is that in a tetrahedron with vertices

annotations.note: A law of sines for tetrahedra is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Space of tetrahedra is 5-dimensional

anchor.section: Trigonometry and the space of all shapes
anchor.snippet: the space of all shapes of tetrahedra is 5-dimensional

annotations.note: The moduli space of tetrahedral shapes is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Law of cosines for tetrahedron

anchor.section: Trigonometry and the space of all shapes
anchor.snippet: The law of cosines for a tetrahedron, which relates the areas of the faces of the tetrahedron to the dihedral angles about a vertex

annotations.note: A law of cosines for tetrahedra is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Interior point face-area identity

anchor.snippet: Let [MATH] be any interior point of a tetrahedron of volume

annotations.note: Interior-point face-area identities are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Equal-area faces perpendicular feet identity

anchor.snippet: feet [MATH] , [MATH] , [MATH] , and [MATH] of the perpendiculars from [MATH] to the faces, and suppose the faces have equal areas

annotations.note: This perpendicular-feet identity for tetrahedra is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Inradius inequality

anchor.snippet: with equality if and only if the tetrahedron is regular

annotations.note: Inradius inequalities (with regularity equality) for tetrahedra are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Inradius from face areas

anchor.snippet: denote the area of each faces, the value of

annotations.note: The inradius-from-face-area formula for tetrahedra is not formalized.

annotations.label: Circumradius formula

anchor.snippet: Denote the circumradius of a tetrahedron as R

annotations.note: Circumradius is defined for any simplex, but the explicit edge-length formula for tetrahedra is not formalized.

annotations.label: Circumcenter via bisector planes

anchor.snippet: The circumcenter of a tetrahedron can be found as intersection of three bisector planes

annotations.note: The circumcenter exists and lies in the affine span, but the bisector-plane intersection characterization is not formalized.

annotations.label: Circumcenter location

anchor.snippet: the circumcenter may not always lie outside of an obtuse tetrahedron

annotations.note: Statements about obtuse-tetrahedron circumcenter location are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Heronian tetrahedra

anchor.snippet: There exist tetrahedra having integer-valued edge lengths, face areas and volume. These are called Heronian tetrahedra

annotations.note: Heronian tetrahedra are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Heronian tetrahedron example

anchor.snippet: One example has one edge of 896, the opposite edge of 990

annotations.note: This specific Heronian tetrahedron example is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Consecutive-integer-edges tetrahedron

anchor.snippet: A tetrahedron can have integer volume and consecutive integers as edges

annotations.note: This consecutive-integer-edges example is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Matchstick puzzle

anchor.snippet: The tetrahedron with regular faces is a solution to an old puzzle asking to form four equilateral triangles using six unbroken matchsticks

annotations.note: Recreational puzzles like the matchstick puzzle are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Tetrahedral bond angle

anchor.snippet: For a perfect tetrahedron, the central angle between any two vertices is arccos

annotations.note: The tetrahedral bond angle arccos(-1/3) is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Thomson and Tammes minimum

anchor.snippet: The vertices of a regular tetrahedron applies as the minimum solution known for

annotations.note: Thomson/Tammes minima for four points on a sphere are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Resistor tetrahedron resistance

anchor.snippet: If six equal resistors are soldered together to form a tetrahedron, then the resistance measured between any two vertices is half that of one resistor

annotations.note: Resistor-network calculations are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Regular tetrahedra do not tessellate

anchor.snippet: Regular tetrahedra cannot tessellate space by themselves

annotations.note: Tessellation impossibility theorems for regular tetrahedra are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: No parallel faces

anchor.snippet: The tetrahedron is unique among the uniform polyhedra in possessing no parallel faces

annotations.note: Parallel-face properties of uniform polyhedra are not formalized in Mathlib.
